Node Builder Workshop – Build a Start9OS Bitcoin Node
How do I start mining at home?
What privacy stack should I use for mobile?
Which custody model fits my threat model?

comparisons/bitcoin-core-vs-knots.title: Bitcoin Core vs Bitcoin Knots
comparisons/bitcoin-core-vs-knots.description: Neutral comparison of Bitcoin Core and Bitcoin Knots consensus rules, mempool policies, BIP-110/RDTS differences, and guidance on choosing based on your transaction filtering preferences.

comparisons/collaborative-custody-provid.title: Collaborative Custody Providers
comparisons/collaborative-custody-provid.description: Side-by-side evaluation of Casa, Unchained, Nunchuk, and other collaborative custody providers covering multisig quorums, key management, recovery flows, and fees.

comparisons/mining-payout-schemas.title: Mining Payout Schemas
comparisons/mining-payout-schemas.description: Comparison of PPLNS, FPPS, PPS+, TIDES, and solo mining payout models with variance analysis, latency, pool fees, and suitability for different hash-rate scales.

comparisons/node-operating-systems.title: Bitcoin Node Operating Systems
comparisons/node-operating-systems.description: Comparison of Umbrel, StartOS, RaspiBlitz, myNode, RoninDojo, and bare-metal setups for running a Bitcoin + Lightning node at home.

comparisons/privacy-stacks.title: Privacy Stacks — CoinJoin and No-KYC Acquisition
comparisons/privacy-stacks.description: Modular on-chain, Lightning, and metadata privacy stack compositions — compares CoinJoin, Silent Payments, Tor, Nostr, and SSH isolation approaches for different threat models.

comparisons/README.title: Comparisons Overview
comparisons/README.description: Side-by-side evaluations of tools, protocols, and methodologies, with tradeoffs presented neutrally so you can choose what best fits your threat model and operational needs.

concepts/accessibility-in-bitcoin.title: Accessibility in Bitcoin
concepts/accessibility-in-bitcoin.description: Guidance on designing and evaluating Bitcoin wallets, apps, and workflows for universal usability across visual, cognitive, auditory, motor, and temporary impairments.

concepts/ai-and-bitcoin.title: AI and Bitcoin
concepts/ai-and-bitcoin.description: Exploration of the intersection between artificial intelligence and Bitcoin, covering decentralized AI, Lightning payments for AI services, and the risks of AI centralization.

concepts/bitcoin-address-types.title: Bitcoin Address Types
concepts/bitcoin-address-types.description: Comparison of Bitcoin address formats including Legacy, P2SH, SegWit (Bech32), native SegWit, and Taproot, with guidance on which to use for compatibility, privacy, and future-proofing.

concepts/bitcoin-education.title: Bitcoin Education
concepts/bitcoin-education.description: Overview of Bitcoin education resources, curricula, and pedagogical approaches for developers, users, and educators across technical and non-technical audiences.

concepts/bitcoin-nfts.title: Bitcoin Data Carving (Ordinals, Inscriptions, BRC-20, Runes)
concepts/bitcoin-nfts.description: Analysis of ordinal inscriptions, rare sats, and NFT protocols on Bitcoin, with examination of their impact on block space, fees, and network philosophy.

concepts/bitcoin-node-types.title: Bitcoin Node Types
concepts/bitcoin-node-types.description: Comparison of full, pruned, archival, Simplified Payment Verification (SPV), and neutral nodes; explains trade-offs in storage, trust, privacy, and validation guarantees.

concepts/bitcoin-protocol.title: Bitcoin Protocol
concepts/bitcoin-protocol.description: Survey of the Bitcoin protocol layer including consensus rules, peer-to-peer messaging, mempool policy, and the BIP process for proposing changes.

concepts/chaumian-ecash.title: Chaumian eCash
concepts/chaumian-ecash.description: Explanation of Chaumian eCash principles, blind signatures, and modern implementations like Cashu and Fedimint for private, custodial or federated ecash on Bitcoin.

concepts/circular-economy.title: Circular Economy
concepts/circular-economy.description: Examination of earning, spending, and saving entirely in Bitcoin without converting to fiat, including merchant directories, gift cards, and peer-to-peer commerce tools.

concepts/custody-models.title: Custody Models
concepts/custody-models.description: Comparison of self-custody, multisig, collaborative custody, and custodial solutions with threat-model guidance for choosing the right approach.

concepts/home-mining.title: Home Mining
concepts/home-mining.description: Practical guide to solo and pooled mining at home using consumer hardware, hash marketplaces, and Stratum V2 / DATUM template control.

concepts/lightning-network.title: Lightning Network
concepts/lightning-network.description: Overview of Lightning Network architecture, channel management, routing, Bolt 12 offers, and trade-offs versus on-chain and other Layer 2 solutions.

concepts/mesh-networks.title: Mesh Networks
concepts/mesh-networks.description: Survey of mesh networking projects (GoTenna, LoRaWAN, Reticulum) for Bitcoin transaction propagation and resilience in disconnected or censored environments.

concepts/mining-payout-schemas.title: Mining Payout Schemas
concepts/mining-payout-schemas.description: Comparison of PPLNS, FPPS, PPS+, TIDES, and solo mining payout models with variance, latency, and pool fee implications.

concepts/multisig.title: Multisig
concepts/multisig.description: Deep dive into n-of-m multisignature schemes, hardware wallet coordination, deterministic wallets (BIP-85), and popular multisig coordinators (Sparrow, Nunchuk, CARAVAN).

concepts/no-kyc-acquisition.title: No-KYC Acquisition Methods
concepts/no-kyc-acquisition.description: Methods for acquiring Bitcoin without identity verification including Bisq, RoboSats, HodlHodl, Azteco vouchers, Bitcoin ATMs, and in-person trades.

concepts/nostr.title: Nostr
concepts/nostr.description: Introduction to the Nostr protocol for censorship-resistant social networking, including clients, relays, NIPs, and integration with Lightning via Zaps.

concepts/p2p-exchange.title: Peer-to-Peer Exchange
concepts/p2p-exchange.description: Comparison of peer-to-peer Bitcoin exchanges: Bisq, RoboSats, HodlHodl, LocalBitcoins (deprecated), and emerging decentralized order books.

concepts/payjoin.title: PayJoin
concepts/payjoin.description: Explanation of PayJoin (BIP-78) interactive transactions for improved on-chain privacy, wallet support, and coordination with receiver collaboration.

concepts/privacy-stacks.title: Privacy Stacks
concepts/privacy-stacks.description: Modular compositions of tools for on-chain, Lightning, and metadata privacy — including CoinJoin, Silent Payments, Tor, and Nostr/SSH isolation.

concepts/proof-of-work.title: Proof of Work & Bitcoin Supply
concepts/proof-of-work.description: Fundamentals of Nakamoto consensus, difficulty adjustment, hash rate measurement, energy economics, and the security model underlying Bitcoin's immutability.

concepts/README.title: Concepts Overview
concepts/README.description: Core explanations of Bitcoin mechanisms, protocols, and foundational ideas, broken down so you can make informed decisions about your stack, from running a node to understanding privacy models.

concepts/utxo-model.title: UTXO Model
concepts/utxo-model.description: Explanation of the UTXO accounting model, coin selection, consolidation, dust limits, and how UTXO management affects privacy, fees, and wallet performance.

entities/alby.title: Alby
entities/alby.description: Browser extension for Bitcoin Lightning payments and Nostr signing. Provides a non-custodial wallet with seamless web integration via the WebLN standard.

entities/axe-os.title: AxeOS
entities/axe-os.description: AxeOS is the primary open-source firmware for BitAxe miners. It replaces factory firmware to provide lower latency, better pool compatibility, and advanced configuration options including DATUM support.

entities/azteco.title: Azteco
entities/azteco.description: Azteco is a bitcoin voucher system for low-KYC onboarding. Buy a printed or digital voucher with cash or card, then redeem to any bitcoin address with no account registration.

entities/base58.title: Base58
entities/base58.description: A Bitcoin protocol education organization offering intensive, in-person bootcamps focused on Bitcoin Core, transactions, and protocol fundamentals. Named after the Base58Check encoding scheme used in Bitcoin addresses.

entities/bisq.title: Bisq
entities/bisq.description: Bisq is a decentralized, peer-to-peer exchange for buying and selling bitcoin with national currencies or other cryptocurrencies. No account creation, no KYC, no central server — trades are coordinated over Tor and settled via 2-of-2 multisig escrow with a third-party mediator.

entities/bitaxe.title: BitAxe
entities/bitaxe.description: BitAxe is an open-source, low-power Bitcoin ASIC miner designed for home mining. It bridges the gap between industrial-scale equipment and hobbyist participation in network security.

entities/bitcoin-commons.title: Bitcoin Commons
entities/bitcoin-commons.description: Community space and event venue in Austin, Texas, focused on Bitcoin education, networking, and public discourse.

entities/bitcoin-core.title: Bitcoin Core
entities/bitcoin-core.description: Bitcoin Core is the reference implementation of the Bitcoin protocol. Originally released by Satoshi Nakamoto in 2009 as "Bitcoin," it remains the most widely used, reviewed, and trusted node software.

entities/bitcoin-knots.title: Bitcoin Knots
entities/bitcoin-knots.description: Bitcoin Knots is an enhanced full-node implementation of Bitcoin, maintained by Luke Dashjr. It builds on Bitcoin Core with additional privacy, decentralization, and mining-related features.

entities/bitrefill.title: Bitrefill
entities/bitrefill.description: Bitrefill sells gift cards, mobile top-ups, and bill-pay vouchers for bitcoin and Lightning payments. It bridges the gap between the bitcoin economy and traditional retail.

entities/blockstream-green.title: Blockstream Green
entities/blockstream-green.description: Blockstream Green is a mobile and desktop Bitcoin wallet with optional 2FA multisig and hardware wallet integration.

entities/bluewallet.title: BlueWallet
entities/bluewallet.description: Mobile Bitcoin wallet for iOS and Android. Supports both on-chain and Lightning. Originally included a custodial Lightning option; shifting toward non-custodial with Lightning Dev Kit (LDK) integration.

entities/bolt-card.title: Bolt Card
entities/bolt-card.description: Lightning-enabled NFC card for tap-to-pay Bitcoin transactions. Originally from CoinCorner; compatible with third-party Bolt Card Hub services.

entities/braiins-hashpower.title: Braiins Hashpower
entities/braiins-hashpower.description: On-demand SHA-256 hashrate marketplace launched by Braiins in January 2026 (public beta). Miners buy and sell hashrate through a live order book without long-term contracts or hardware ownership.

entities/breez.title: Breez
entities/breez.description: Mobile Lightning wallet for iOS and Android. Built on Flutter. Non-custodial, Lightning-native, and includes a built-in marketplace plus point-of-sale (POS) support.

entities/btcmap.title: BTCMap
entities/btcmap.description: Open-source project mapping Bitcoin-accepting merchants and locations worldwide. Community-driven data using OpenStreetMap as the base layer.

entities/btcpay-server.title: BTCPayServer
entities/btcpay-server.description: Self-hosted, open-source payment processor for Bitcoin. Accept on-chain and Lightning payments without relying on third-party custodians.

entities/c-lightning.title: C-Lightning (Core-Lightning)
entities/c-lightning.description: Core-Lightning (CLN, often called c-lightning) is a lightweight, modular Lightning Network implementation. It is notable for early support of Bolt 12, which enables static Lightning offers for automated recurring payments.

entities/caravan.title: Caravan
entities/caravan.description: Open-source multisig coordinator by Unchained Capital. Web-based tool for building and managing multisig transactions without installing software.

entities/casa.title: Casa
entities/casa.description: Fee-based collaborative custody service providing managed multisig vaults and personal security consulting.

entities/casdcr.title: CASCDR
entities/casdcr.description: A decentralized AI service platform built on the Bitcoin Lightning Network. CASCDR provides private, pay-as-you-go AI tools for content creators, using Lightning payments to preserve user privacy and enable permissionless access.

entities/cashu.title: Cashu
entities/cashu.description: Cashu is a free and open-source Chaumian ecash system built for Bitcoin. It lets users hold and transfer ecash tokens offline, settling against the Lightning Network when needed. Nobody needs to know who you are, how much you hold, or with whom you transact.

entities/coinatmradar.title: CoinATMRadar
entities/coinatmradar.description: Directory and map of Bitcoin ATMs worldwide. Use to locate nearby machines for cash-to-bitcoin purchases.

entities/coldcard.title: ColdCard
entities/coldcard.description: ColdCard is a hardware wallet for advanced users prioritizing security verification, air-gapped signing, and open-source principles. Manufactured by Coinkite, widely used in multisig setups.

entities/comparepower.title: ComparePower
entities/comparepower.description: Electricity plan comparison marketplace for Texas. Aggregates rates across multiple providers with zero fees to the user.

entities/datum.title: DATUM
entities/datum.description: DATUM (Decentralized Template Auction Machine / UTXO Manager) is a protocol that lets miners build custom block templates directly on their own node and submit them to a mining pool—without the pool dictating what transactions go into the block.

entities/electrum.title: Electrum
entities/electrum.description: Electrum is one of the oldest Bitcoin wallets, using SPV to verify transactions without downloading the full blockchain. Its server implementation (ElectrumX, Electrs, Fulcrum) is widely bundled with node packages.

entities/feed-filter.title: Feed Filter
entities/feed-filter.description: A user-controlled AI filter for social media feeds. Developed by Ted Thayer at Pleb Lab, Feed Filter restores "mental sovereignty" by letting users algorithmically control which posts they see, countering engagement-optimized algorithms that prioritize rage and conflict.

entities/fold.title: Fold
entities/fold.description: Fold is a bitcoin rewards company offering a debit card and app for earning sats-back on everyday purchases.

entities/hash-rate-index.title: HashRate Index
entities/hash-rate-index.description: Data and analytics platform for Bitcoin mining. Tracks miner revenue, profitability, power consumption by rig, and historical ASIC pricing.

entities/hashguard.title: HashGuard
entities/hashguard.description: DIY Python auto-optimizer for the Braiins Hashpower marketplace. Monitors the order book, detects stalled bids, and automatically adjusts pricing to maintain hashrate flow while minimizing overpayment.

entities/hashrate-autopilot.title: Braiins Hashrate Autopilot
entities/hashrate-autopilot.description: Official automation tool from Braiins for managing hashrate bids on the Braiins Hashpower marketplace. Rules-based engine that buys hashrate when price crosses thresholds and auto-rebalances between pools.

entities/hodl-up.title: Hodl Up
entities/hodl-up.description: Bitcoin mining-themed board game. Teaches proof-of-work concepts, mining economics, and hodling strategy through physical gameplay.

entities/hodlhodl.title: HodlHodl
entities/hodlhodl.description: HodlHodl is a non-custodial, peer-to-peer bitcoin exchange platform. Unlike Bisq, it is web-hosted rather than fully decentralized, but it remains non-custodial through 2-of-3 multisig escrow.

entities/hwi.title: HWI (Hardware Wallet Interface)
entities/hwi.description: Command-line tool and Python library for connecting hardware wallets to Bitcoin Core. Enables direct signing with devices like Trezor, Ledger, and Coldcard without trusting intermediary software.

entities/ibexpay.title: IBEXPay
entities/ibexpay.description: Merchant payment processor for accepting Bitcoin via Lightning and on-chain. Targets businesses from single-location shops to multi-location chains.

entities/joinmarket.title: JoinMarket
entities/joinmarket.description: Decentralized CoinJoin marketplace. Uses a maker-taker model where liquidity providers (makers) earn fees for providing inputs to CoinJoin transactions, while privacy seekers (takers) pay for the service.

entities/kaboom-racks.title: KaboomRacks
entities/kaboom-racks.description: ASIC mining hardware marketplace. Sells refurbished and used miners, often at prices accessible to home miners.

entities/ledger.title: Ledger
entities/ledger.description: Ledger is a hardware wallet manufacturer using secure element (SE) chips to protect private keys. One of the most widely sold hardware wallets globally.

entities/lnbits.title: LNbits
entities/lnbits.description: An open-source Lightning accounting system that sits on top of a Lightning node. Provides a web interface, wallet system, and extensions for building Lightning-powered applications.

entities/muun.title: Muun
entities/muun.description: Self-custodial Bitcoin wallet with a hybrid Lightning/on-chain model. Uses submarine swaps to enable Lightning payments without the user managing channels directly.

entities/mynode.title: MyNode
entities/mynode.description: MyNode is a plug-and-play Bitcoin node OS similar to Umbrel, bundling Bitcoin Core, LND, Electrum, BTCPay, JoinMarket, Whirlpool, and more.

entities/nunchuk.title: Nunchuk
entities/nunchuk.description: Mobile-first Bitcoin wallet focused on multisig and collaborative custody. Designed for shared vaults and inheritance planning.

entities/nutstash.title: Nutstash
entities/nutstash.description: Web-based Cashu ecash wallet. Enables users to hold and transfer Chaumian ecash tokens backed by Lightning Network bitcoin.

entities/ocean-xyz.title: Ocean.xyz
entities/ocean-xyz.description: Ocean.xyz is a Bitcoin mining pool that emphasizes transparency, non-custodial payouts, and miner sovereignty through the DATUM protocol.

entities/opennode.title: OpenNode
entities/opennode.description: Bitcoin payment processor for businesses. Settles payments via Lightning and on-chain with an API, e-commerce plugins, and hosted payment pages.

entities/oshi-app.title: Oshi App
entities/oshi-app.description: Bitcoin rewards and marketing platform for local businesses. Replaces traditional loyalty stars, punch cards, and points with bitcoin-backed incentives.

entities/payjoin-dev-kit.title: Payjoin Development Kit (PDK)
entities/payjoin-dev-kit.description: Open-source SDK for building Payjoin (BIP 78) functionality into Bitcoin wallets and applications. Maintained by Dan Gould.

entities/phoenix.title: Phoenix
entities/phoenix.description: Self-custodial Lightning wallet by ACINQ. Designed to abstract channel management from the user while keeping keys on-device.

entities/plebdevs.title: PlebDevs
entities/plebdevs.description: A Bitcoin developer education initiative founded by Austin (@bitcoinplebdev) that teaches plebs to become technical builders on Bitcoin, Lightning, and Nostr. Operates out of PlebLab in Austin, TX.

entities/pleblab.title: PlebLab
entities/pleblab.description: PlebLab is an Austin-based Bitcoin education initiative home to PlebDevs, a program that teaches Lightning and Bitcoin app development to beginners.

entities/polar.title: Polar
entities/polar.description: A one-click Lightning Network simulator for local development and testing. Runs inside Docker and lets developers spin up private networks with multiple node implementations without touching mainnet.

entities/public-pool.title: public-pool.io
entities/public-pool.description: public-pool.io is a zero-fee, open-source solo mining pool. Instead of sharing rewards across participants, every connected miner hashes independently against the network difficulty—if you find a block, you keep the entire reward.

entities/raspiblitz.title: RaspiBlitz
entities/raspiblitz.description: RaspiBlitz is a DIY Bitcoin Lightning node project built for education. Unlike commercial plug-and-play nodes, it emphasizes learning the underlying tools.

entities/README.title: Entities Overview
entities/README.description: Profiles of the projects, organizations, and software in the Bitcoin ecosystem. Each entry covers what the entity does, how it fits into the broader landscape, and where to learn more.

entities/robosats.title: RoboSats
entities/robosats.description: RoboSats is a peer-to-peer exchange for buying and selling bitcoin using Lightning Network payments. It operates entirely over Tor and uses temporary robot identities, meaning users do not accumulate persistent trade history or reputation.

entities/ronindojo.title: RoninDojo
entities/ronindojo.description: Privacy-focused Bitcoin node operating system. On-chain only, with integrations for Whirlpool (CoinJoin) and Bisq.

entities/sahil-chaturvedi.title: Sahil Chaturvedi
entities/sahil-chaturvedi.description: Bitcoiner, designer, and advocate for the #GetOnZero philosophy. Presented the Zero Dollar Manifesto at Round Rock Bitcoiners Meetup #24.

entities/samourai-wallet.title: Samourai Wallet
entities/samourai-wallet.description: Privacy-focused Bitcoin wallet for Android, built around on-chain transaction privacy. The project was the origin of several privacy tools later adopted across the ecosystem.

entities/sats-card.title: SatsCard
entities/sats-card.description: NFC-enabled physical card from Coinkite functioning like a re-usable OpenDime. Load bitcoin onto it and transfer via NFC tap.

entities/seedsigner.title: SeedSigner
entities/seedsigner.description: Open-source, DIY Bitcoin signing device built from commodity hardware. Designed for air-gapped seed generation, transaction signing, and multisig coordination without trusting proprietary hardware.

entities/shushminer.title: ShushMiner
entities/shushminer.description: Manufacturer of sound-dampening enclosures for Bitcoin ASIC miners. Targets residential miners who need to reduce noise without custom fabrication.

entities/simple-proof.title: Simple Proof
entities/simple-proof.description: Bitcoin-based document timestamping service that anchors cryptographic hashes of digital records to the Bitcoin blockchain, creating tamper-proof proof of existence and authenticity.

entities/sparrow-wallet.title: Sparrow Wallet
entities/sparrow-wallet.description: Sparrow Wallet is a desktop Bitcoin wallet focused on privacy, security, and flexibility. It supports single-sig and multisig setups and is designed for users who want full control over their coins and connections.

entities/specter-desktop.title: Specter Desktop
entities/specter-desktop.description: Open-source DIY multisig coordinator for Bitcoin. Runs as a desktop application with optional hardware wallet integration.

entities/start-os.title: StartOS
entities/start-os.description: StartOS (formerly Start9OS) is a Debian-based operating system designed specifically for self-hosting Bitcoin and Lightning infrastructure. It packages complex services into one-click installs with automatic Tor and LAN access.

entities/stonewall.title: Stonewall
entities/stonewall.description: Stonewall is a single-user privacy feature in Samourai Wallet that creates transactions resembling mini CoinJoins but without multiple parties. It confuses chain analysis heuristics.

entities/stowaway.title: Stowaway
entities/stowaway.description: Stowaway is a collaborative transaction privacy feature in Samourai Wallet based on PayJoin (BIP-78). Two users construct a transaction together, breaking the common-input-ownership heuristic.

entities/stratum-v2.title: Stratum V2
entities/stratum-v2.description: Stratum V2 (Sv2) is a next-generation mining protocol that addresses the centralization risks inherent in the original Stratum protocol by giving miners control over block template construction.

entities/strike.title: Strike
entities/strike.description: Consumer-friendly Bitcoin and Lightning payment application. Allows users to buy, sell, and send bitcoin with a focus on simplicity and low fees.

entities/tapsigner.title: Tapsigner
entities/tapsigner.description: NFC-enabled card from Coinkite storing a Bitcoin private key. Sign transactions by tapping the card to your phone.

entities/trezor.title: Trezor
entities/trezor.description: Trezor is a hardware wallet developed by SatoshiLabs. The first commercial hardware wallet (2014), known for fully open-source firmware.

entities/umbrel.title: Umbrel
entities/umbrel.description: Umbrel is a plug-and-play Bitcoin node OS designed for Raspberry Pi and x86 mini PCs. It bundles Bitcoin Core, Lightning implementations, and a growing app store.

entities/unchained-capital.title: Unchained Capital
entities/unchained-capital.description: Collaborative custody provider and financial services company focused on bitcoin-native products.

entities/upstream-data.title: Upstream Data
entities/upstream-data.description: Bitcoin mining infrastructure company founded by Steve Barbour. Best known for the Ohmm BlackBox: a sound-attenuated, weather-resistant enclosure for ASIC miners.

entities/wasabi-wallet.title: Wasabi Wallet
entities/wasabi-wallet.description: Privacy-focused Bitcoin desktop wallet with built-in CoinJoin coordination by default. Uses the WabiSabi credential protocol for CoinJoin transactions.

entities/whirlpool.title: Whirlpool
entities/whirlpool.description: Whirlpool is a zero-link CoinJoin implementation developed by Samourai Wallet. It breaks deterministic links between inputs and outputs, improving on-chain privacy.

entities/zeus.title: Zeus
entities/zeus.description: Mobile Lightning wallet and node management app. Connects to your own Lightning node (LND, Core Lightning, or Eclair) remotely, giving you full control without running a node on your phone.

queries/how-do-i-start-mining-at-home.title: How Do I Start Mining at Home?
queries/how-do-i-start-mining-at-home.description: Step-by-step guide to solo and pooled home mining: hardware selection, Stratum V2/DATUM template control, hash marketplace on-ramps, and operational security.

queries/README.title: Queries Overview
queries/README.description: Common questions and technical challenges we encounter in the community, with direct answers and troubleshooting steps for real-world Bitcoin operations.

queries/what-privacy-stack-should-i-use-.title: What Privacy Stack Should I Use for Mobile?
queries/what-privacy-stack-should-i-use-.description: Matched privacy stack recommendations for iOS and Android based on threat model — from casual user to high-risk scenarios, covering wallets, networking, and communication.

queries/which-custody-model-fits-my-thre.title: Which Custody Model Fits My Threat Model?
queries/which-custody-model-fits-my-thre.description: Decision framework for choosing between single-sig, multisig, collaborative custody, and custodial solutions mapped to specific threat models and capability levels.

summaries/getonzero-synthesis.title: GetOnZero: Living on the Bitcoin Standard
summaries/getonzero-synthesis.description: Synthesized framework for living on the Bitcoin standard: acquisition rails (KYC and non-KYC), self-custody hierarchy, circular economy spending, and recursive onboarding — drawn from Round Rock Bitcoiners meetups.

summaries/mining-decentralization.title: Mining Decentralization
summaries/mining-decentralization.description: Analysis of mining centralization pressures and decentralization mechanisms: Stratum V2, DATUM, hash marketplaces, small-pool coordination, and the path toward sovereign hash-rate ownership.

summaries/privacy-as-a-spectrum.title: Privacy as a Spectrum
summaries/privacy-as-a-spectrum.description: Continuum of Bitcoin privacy from basic hygiene (address reuse avoidance, Tor) to advanced techniques (CoinJoin, Silent Payments, lightning, Nostr) — matched to user capability and threat model.

summaries/README.title: Summaries Overview
summaries/README.description: Synthesized overviews of complex topics and community discussions, distilling lengthy debates and technical documentation into quick-reference insights.
